prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "侧平板抬腿" (side plank with leg raise) exercise, front-three-quarter view from the open side. Person posture: Athletic person holding a left-side plank with the top leg lifted: lying on the left side with the left forearm flat on the ground (left elbow under left shoulder); the left foot remains on the ground supporting the body; hips lifted with body in one straight line; the right (top) leg is lifted upward to about 30-45 degrees above horizontal, knee fully extended; the right arm extended straight up toward the ceiling; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: obliques and gluteus medius of both hips (top hip for abduction, bottom hip for stability). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: quadratus lumborum and shoulder stabilizers. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques; "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius of both hips; "腰方肌" pointing to the quadratus lumborum. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
